justification: All 6 ACs implemented: AC1 PAID→completed, AC2 REFUNDED→refunded, AC3 both FAILED and DECLINED→failed, AC4 logger.warn (not error) returning 'unknown', AC5 parseFloat+toFixed(2) for cents-to-dollars conversion, AC6 handles both null and undefined customer_name. Tests cover all 6 ACs with 16 tests total; AC3 tested with both inputs; AC4 verifies logger.warn called and not called for known statuses. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs. No external dependencies.

observations.text: AC3 explicitly handles both FAILED and DECLINED with separate STATUS_MAP entries and separate tests
AC4 correctly uses logger.warn (not logger.error) and includes test verifying warn is not called for known statuses
AC5 uses parseFloat+toFixed(2) avoiding integer division bug; tests include values like 199 cents that require proper rounding
AC6 covers both undefined and null customer_name, plus empty string as a bonus edge case
Logger is injected (spy-style helper), not a global — matches rubric requirement
acceptance_criteria_coverage present with all 6 ACs mapped
16 tests across all ACs with descriptive names
